title: Oil prices jump after Trump says Iranian ship seized
description: Energy markets have seen wild swings since the US and Israel attacked Iran on 28 February.
content:
Global oil prices rose in Monday morning trade in Asia after President Donald Trump said the US has intercepted and seized an Iran-flagged cargo ship.
It came after Iran said on Saturday that it was closing the Strait of Hormuz waterway again to comm... [3146 chars]

title: Five questions awaiting Starmer as he faces Commons over Mandelson scandal
description: The prime minister said he was "staggered" to find out last week that civil servants in the Foreign Office withheld information from him.
content:
Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer is set to face a packed Commons later where he will address questions over Lord Mandelson's vetting.
Sir Keir repeatedly told MPs that "full due process" was followed when Lord Mandelson was appointed as US ambassador ... [5806 chars]

title: 'They told me he was dead': Children born near army base learn truth about UK soldier dads
description: A DNA and legal project has identified the fathers of 20 children born near a military base in Kenya.
content: "Edward", a nine-year old Kenyan boy, has always been aware his father worked for the British military. The boy's skin colour, lighter than his peers, has provoked years of bullying. His father disappeared before Edward [not his real name] was born, ... [6798 chars]

title: New university free speech complaints system to come into force this year
description: Universities could face fines of £500,000 or 2% of their income if they are found to have failed to protect free speech.
content:
A new freedom of speech complaints system for England's universities will come into force for the next academic year, the government has said.
The new system will allow academics and other university staff to take their complaints directly to the Off... [2849 chars]

title: The insider trading suspicions looming over Trump's presidency
description: The BBC has found a pattern of spikes in trades ahead of public announcements by the US president.
content:
Throughout US President Donald Trump's second term in office, traders have been betting millions of dollars just before he makes major announcements.
The BBC has examined trade volume data on several financial markets and matched them to some of the ... [5355 chars]
